task_completion.description: Set Stellarium date and time to November 1, 2023, at 06:00 UTC
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's Date and Time window or bottom status bar clearly showing the date '2023-11-01' (or 2023/11/01) and time '06:00:00'.

task_completion.description: Disable atmosphere and ground, and enable equatorial grid and planet labels
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium sky view showing a black space background (no blue atmosphere), no landscape/ground at the bottom, visible curved lines representing the equatorial grid, and text labels for celestial bodies.

task_completion.description: Capture a high-magnification screenshot of Jupiter showing its disk and moons
task_completion.visual_evidence: A heavily zoomed-in view of Jupiter in Stellarium (or a saved image file) where Jupiter's spherical disk is clearly visible along with nearby labeled points of light representing its moons.

task_completion.description: Capture a high-magnification screenshot of Saturn showing its rings and moons
task_completion.visual_evidence: A heavily zoomed-in view of Saturn in Stellarium (or a saved image file) where Saturn's rings are clearly visible along with nearby labeled points of light representing its moons.

task_completion.description: Create the astrometry answer key text file with the required information
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with a file named 'astrometry_key.txt' containing the reference date (2023-11-01), Jupiter's four Galilean moons (Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto), and Saturn's largest moon (Titan).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Stellarium application to view the planets
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the distinct Stellarium user interface, rendering style, and toolbars, rather than a web browser open to an image search or Wikipedia.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a text editor or visible terminal to create the answer key
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor (like gedit or mousepad) or a visible terminal editor (like nano/vim) should be seen on screen with the typed answers, rather than the file being generated invisibly via background scripts.

integrity.description: Verify screenshots were saved to the correct directory
integrity.visual_evidence: A file manager window open to '/home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/' showing the captured images, or an on-screen Stellarium notification confirming the screenshot was saved to that specific path.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Date: 2023-11-01. Target Time: 06:00:00 UTC. Required Moons in Answer Key: Io, Europa, Ganymede, Callisto (Jupiter) and Titan (Saturn). Screenshots Directory: /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium. Lab Key Path: /home/ga/Desktop/astrometry_key.txt.
